contents.value: **define** — Scheme keyword.
**define-syntax** — Scheme keyword.
**define-record-type** — Scheme keyword.
**lambda** — Scheme keyword.
**let** — Scheme keyword.
**car** — Built-in function/command in Scheme.
**cdr** — Built-in function/command in Scheme.
**cons** — Built-in function/command in Scheme.
**list** — Built-in function/command in Scheme.
**append** — Built-in function/command in Scheme.
A **function** is a reusable block of code that performs a specific task.
A **variable** stores a value that can be referenced and modified.
A **loop** repeats a block of code while a condition is true or for each item in a collection.
A **conditional** executes different code branches based on a boolean test.
A **string** is a sequence of characters used for text.
An **array** (or list) is an ordered collection of elements.
A **dictionary** (hash/map) stores key-value pairs for fast lookup.
A **class** defines a blueprint for creating objects with attributes and methods.
A **module** is a file containing reusable code that can be imported.
An **exception** signals an error condition that can be caught and handled.
A **closure** captures variables from its enclosing scope.
An **iterator** yields values one at a time from a sequence.
A **generator** lazily produces values using yield.
A **decorator** wraps a function to extend its behavior.

```scheme
(let* ((var1 expr1) (var2 expr2) ...) body ...) -> value
```
Sequential binding form where each variable binding is visible to subsequent binding expressions, allowing later bindings to reference earlier ones in the same let block.

```scheme
(letrec ((var1 expr1) (var2 expr2) ...) body ...) -> value
```
Recursive binding form where all variables are in scope for all expressions, enabling mutually recursive procedure definitions within a local binding block.

```scheme
(cond (test1 expr1 ...) (test2 expr2 ...) ... (else expr ...)) -> value
```
Multi-way conditional expression that evaluates test clauses in order and returns the value of the expression sequence for the first true test, with an optional else clause.

```scheme
(map procedure list1 list2 ...) -> list
```
Applies a procedure element-wise to corresponding elements of one or more lists and returns a new list of the results, preserving the order of elements.

```scheme
(fold-left procedure init list) -> value
```
Reduces a list from left to right by applying a binary procedure to an accumulator (starting with init) and each element, returning the final accumulated value.

```scheme
(call-with-current-continuation procedure) -> value
```
Captures the current continuation as a first-class escape procedure and passes it to the given procedure, enabling non-local exits, coroutines, and advanced control flow patterns.

```scheme
(define-syntax name (syntax-rules (literals ...) (pattern template) ...)) -> macro
```
Defines a hygienic macro transformer using pattern matching rules, where each pattern is matched against use-site forms and the corresponding template generates the expansion.

```scheme
(values val1 val2 ...) -> multiple values
```
Returns multiple values from a procedure, which can be received by call-with-values or let-values, enabling functions to produce more than one result efficiently.

```scheme
(apply procedure arg1 ... args-list) -> value
```
Calls a procedure with arguments taken from a list, optionally preceded by individual arguments, effectively spreading the list elements as positional parameters.

```scheme
(for-each procedure list1 list2 ...) -> void
```
Applies a procedure to corresponding elements of one or more lists in order purely for side effects, guaranteed to process elements sequentially unlike map.

```scheme
(dynamic-wind before thunk after) -> value
```
Executes thunk between before and after procedures, guaranteeing that before runs on entry and after on exit even when continuations transfer control in or out.

```scheme
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```scheme
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```scheme
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```scheme

```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```scheme

```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).
